body: The standard rate of VAT is 20%. Most goods and services are charged at the standard rate of 20%. You should charge this rate unless the goods or services are classed as reduced or zero-rated. You can reclaim the VAT you've paid on business-related goods and services. The VAT you pay is usually the difference between any VAT you've paid to other businesses, and the VAT you've charged your customers. If you've charged more VAT than you've paid, you must pay the difference to HMRC. If you've paid more VAT than you've charged, HMRC will usually repay you the difference.
heading: How VAT works
